bonuses.leadin: The Paper Partition System was designed to provide temporary housing for victims of earthquakes. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The Paper Partition System was designed to provide temporary housing for victims of earthquakes. For 10 points each:
parts: Buildings that have withstood earthquakes include Tokyo’s Imperial Hotel, which was designed by this American architect. This architect later designed Fallingwater.
Wright designed the Imperial Hotel in a building of this name, his home and studio. Another building by Wright called “[this name] West” is located in Arizona.

Japanese architect Shigeru Ban developed the Paper Partition System and designed one of these buildings made out of cardboard in New Zealand. The “Crystal [one of these buildings]” was named for its use of glass by architect Philip Johnson.

answers_sanitized: cathedrals [accept Cardboard Cathedral or Crystal Cathedral; prompt on churches]
